hypothesis:
Given that reaction template frequency in the training set strongly correlates with model performance across all architectures, and that rare templates (<10 occurrences) show dramatically reduced accuracy despite potentially representing chemically valid transformations:

**Hypothesis:** Augmenting the training set with synthetically generated reactions for rare templates (frequency 1-10) using rule-based chemistry engines or quantum chemical calculations will disproportionately improve top-k accuracy for underrepresented reaction classes without requiring additional experimental data collection, thereby mitigating class imbalance effects more efficiently than random oversampling or class reweighting strategies.
